title: Changes in Money and Quasi-Money and Factors Affecting Changes, Iran, 1964/65-1970/71 (World Bank, 1971)
source_org: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development (World Bank), data sourced by the Bank from Bank Markazi Iran
extraction_method: MANUAL TRANSCRIPTION, not bulk download. Source document is data/raw/world-bank-archives-iran/historical-documents/1971_cep_vol7_statistical_annex.pdf (Iran - Current economic position and prospects, Vol. 7 of 7: Statistical annex, May 18 1971), a scanned typewriter-era World Bank report. Table 6.2 is on PDF page 70. Rendered at 300 DPI via `pdftoppm -r 300 -f 69 -l 70 -png` and read directly from the rendered PNG using the Read tool; print quality on this page was good and no further crop/zoom passes were needed. Unlike the adjacent Table 6.1 (same source PDF, page 69), this table's own printed note explicitly states 'Due to rounding, components do not always add up to totals', so component-sum cross-checks were not used as a correctness test here (minor non-additivity is expected and not a transcription error) -- transcription instead relied on direct high-resolution visual reads, cross-referenced against footnote markers to confirm which cells carry which footnote. All four footnotes are preserved in these notes rather than in the CSV (to keep the CSV machine-readable): 1/ (Foreign Assets 1964/65 and 1968/69/Claims on Public Sector /National Government/Official entities 1964/65-1965/66) includes the impact of the Rls. 1.6 billion SDR allocation plus a Rls. 6.3 billion advance payment from the oil Consortium; 2/ (National Government net, 1969/70) includes the impact of the government's receipt of that same Rls. 6.3 billion Consortium advance payment; 3/ (Foreign Assets and National Government net, First 6 Months 1970/71) includes the impact of repayment of that Consortium advance (Rls. 5.8 billion); 4/ (Foreign Assets and Domestic Assets, Proj. 1970/71; Other Items net, Proj. 1970/71) excludes the impact of a second SDR allocation. For the 'Proj. 1970/71' column only, the source itself brackets 'Currency in Circulation' and 'Demand Deposits' together as a single combined figure (10.2) rather than printing them separately (the same bracketing convention seen in Table 6.1 for 1968/69-1969/70), and several other sub-line splits (National Government/Official entities; Capital Accounts; SDR Allocation; Other Items) are simply not printed for that projection column at all -- both are preserved as blank/combined rather than fabricated. No value was interpolated, estimated, or fabricated.
time_coverage: 1964/65-1969/70 actuals, plus a 1970/71 projection and first-6-months actuals for 1969/70 vs 1970/71 (Iranian fiscal year, ending March 20)
frequency: annual (fiscal year, year-over-year change) plus semi-annual (first 6 months) comparison columns
units: billion rials, year-over-year change (flow, not level)
license_terms: World Bank Archives — public disclosure authorized document
notes: Source: Table 6.2, page 70 of the Statistical Annex, source line 'Bank Markazi Iran'. This is the flow/change companion to Table 6.1 (Monetary Survey, same document page 69, level data, already extracted as dataset wb1971-monetary-survey-1963-1970) -- Table 6.1 gives year-end stock levels while this table gives the year-over-year changes and decomposes them into contributing factors (foreign assets, domestic assets/credit to private sector, credit to public sector split by national government vs. official entities, and other items). The 'Changes in Percent' rows give the annual growth rates directly (e.g. Money Supply and Quasi-Money grew 13-21% per year across 1964/65-1969/70, accelerating notably in 1967/68-1968/69 to 20-21%, coinciding with the also-visible acceleration in Time and Savings Deposits growth in the same years per Table 6.1). Part of a systematic effort to extract Pahlavi-era budget/money-supply/banking data from primary-source World Bank PDFs in data/raw/world-bank-archives-iran/historical-documents/, following the same visual-verification methodology as the wb1960-*, wb1971-ordinary-budget-*, wb1971-government-revenue-*, and wb1971-monetary-survey-* datasets in this same directory.
